meaning: adj.(西班牙东部的)马略卡岛的,马略卡人的n.马略卡人,马略卡语
examples: Finally we found a Spanish restaurant, where we had seafood and some more delicious Majorcan food.
All this global gabble is best summarized by two signs in a Majorcan shop entrance: "English well talking"and"Here speeching American".
Star gazers can find Nadal's asteroid between Mars and Jupiter and the 22-year-old has himself been invited to the Majorcan observatory to view it himself.
It was really upsetting and the Majorcan people are so lovely they do not deserve this atrocity.
Suddenly, the Majorcan is on top and sends over a darting inside-out forehand that Murray can't return.
